data.title: Strategy Stock Pops on Saylor's Biggest BTC Purchase Since July 2026
data.description:
While most companies are cautious about the crypto market, bitcoin-winter">one firm is doubling down. Strategy stock (MSTR) jumped in early trading as the company recently revealed that it bought another 13,627 Bitcoin in just one week. This is the largest amount the company has purchased at once since July 2025. This move cements the company's place as the world's biggest corporate owner of Bitcoin (BTC-USD), bringing its total collection to nearly 687,410 coins.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

How Strategy Finds the Cash

You might wonder where a company gets over a billion dollars to buy digital currency. Strategy uses a unique method called an "at-the-market" program. Instead of using its own savings, the company sells its own stock to investors and immediately uses that money to buy more Bitcoin. Last week, they raised about $1.25 billion through this method. It is an interesting cycle: the more Bitcoin they buy, the more interest they get from investors, which allows them to sell more stock and buy even more coins.

Strategy Buys the Highs with Confidence

What makes this purchase interesting is the price. Strategy bought this latest batch of Bitcoin at an average price of about $91,519 per coin. This is much higher than the $75,000 average price they paid for their earlier coins. Even though the company recently reported some paper losses because the market dipped at the end of last year, its leaders seem completely unbothered. They are focused on the long term, betting that Bitcoin will be worth far more in the years to come.

Strategy Leads a Corporate Movement

Strategy isn't the only one playing this game anymore. Since they started their journey in 2020, over 100 other public companies have followed their lead and added Bitcoin to their balance sheets. Collectively, these top 100 companies now hold more than 1 million Bitcoin. Strategy is essentially the blueprint for how a modern business can use digital assets to grow, and its latest billion-dollar move shows that the company believes the best is yet to come.

Is Strategy a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Turning to TipRanks, analyst data shows Wall Street remains firmly optimistic about Strategy (MSTR). In total, 14 analysts have weighed in over the past three months, and the consensus rating sits at Strong Buy. Out of these calls, 12 analysts call the stock a Buy, two say a Hold, and none recommend a Sell.

The average 12-month MSTR price target comes in at $453.17, which implies roughly 180.7% upside from the recent close.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Tesla's (TSLA) China Sales Fell in 2025, but Ended on a Strong Note
data.description:
EV maker Tesla (TSLA) sold 625,698 vehicles in China in 2025, a 4.8% decline from the year before, as competition increased and the company underwent a major production change. As a result, its market share fell from 6% in 2024 to 4.9% in 2025, placing it in fifth place, according to CNEV Post. A big part of this slowdown came from Tesla switching production to the new Model Y, which caused temporary shutdowns at its Shanghai factory early in the year. Due to those shutdowns, Tesla had fewer vehicles available to sell, not just in China but also for export.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In fact, exports from Giga Shanghai fell to 226,034 vehicles, down nearly 13% year-over-year. However, even with that decline, China remained one of Tesla's most important markets by accounting for 38.24% of its global deliveries of 1.64 million vehicles in 2025. Still, it is not yet clear how much of the export drop was due to the Model Y changeover versus other market pressures. Nevertheless, Tesla ended the year on a strong note. According to the China Passenger Car Association, the company delivered a record 93,843 vehicles in December, which was 13.2% higher than a year earlier and 28.3% higher than November.

This late surge came as Tesla prioritized domestic deliveries so that customers could take advantage of favorable tax policies. As a result, the company achieved a 7% share of China's new energy vehicle market and a 12% share of the battery-electric vehicle segment in December. For the full year, Tesla China sold 851,732 vehicles on a wholesale basis, including 97,171 in December, while Model 3 wholesale sales fell 13.1% to 312,738 units and Model Y wholesale sales slipped 3.2% to 538,994 units.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 13 Buys, 10 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $405.94 per share implies 9.5% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Former Fed Chairs Warn Eroding Fed Autonomy Carries 'Highly Negative Consequences'
data.description:
On Monday, several former Fed Chairs and Treasury Secretaries, including Janet Yellen, Ben Bernanke, and Alan Greenspan, issued a joint statement in response to the Department of Justice's investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The reported criminal inquiry into Federal Reserve Chair Jay Powell is an unprecedented attempt to use prosecutorial attacks to undermine that independence," read the statement.

Why is Powell Being Investigated?

The statement added that waning Fed independence could result in "highly negative consequences" for inflation and the economy, similar to previous situations seen in emerging markets.

The Justice Department's investigation stems from Powell's oversight of the central bank's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. In a response to the probe, Powell framed the renovation as a pretext for political attacks on the Fed's independence amid a feud with Trump, who has repeatedly called for lower rates.

data.title: Bank of America Stays Bullish on Amazon Stock (AMZN), Says 'AWS AI Sentiment Key Driver in 2026'
data.description:
Bank of America analyst Justin Post reiterated a Buy rating on Amazon (AMZN) stock with a price target of $303. The analyst noted that AMZN stock gained only 5% in 2025, underperforming the 16% and 20% rise in the S&P 500 (SPX) and Nasdaq (NDX). Nevertheless, the analyst remains optimistic about AMZN stock as he expects improvement in the company's Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud unit and said "AWS AI sentiment key driver in 2026."

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Key Reasons for Bank of America Analyst's Bullish Stance

Post noted that Amazon's Retail business continued to improve in 2025, with slight share gains and expanding margins. However, weaker sentiment around AWS's AI positioning dragged down the forward EV/EBITDA multiple by about 15%. The 5-star analyst believes that AI sentiment could improve in 2026. His optimism is based on:

  • Expectation of acceleration in AWS growth, driven by enhanced capacity
  • Recent AWS leadership changes that could fuel a change in the cloud unit's AI positioning
  • Rapid growth in Rufus (AI-powered shopping assistant) usage could pave the way for an agent-led retail future.

Post added that if Amazon's proprietary tech, namely large language model (LLM) and Trainium chips, continues to improve relative to competitors, AWS's AI positioning could gain a significant edge over the long term as enterprises prioritize cost-efficient AI inference.

The top-rated analyst noted that at a price of $250, AMZN stock trades at 11x the Street's 2027 EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization) estimate and 25x GAAP EPS (earnings per share) estimate. The analyst contends that while Amazon's valuation reflects uncertainty around AWS's competitive positioning, there is scope for improvement if the cloud unit's top-line growth accelerates and the company enhances its AI capabilities.

Is AMZN a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Amazon stock scores Wall Street's Strong Buy consensus rating based on 46 Buys versus only one Hold rating. The average AMZN stock price target of $294.69 implies 19.1% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Salesforce Stock (CRM): Goldman Sees Strong Upside as Agentforce Takes Center Stage
data.description:
Goldman Sachs analysts have assumed coverage of Salesforce (CRM) with a Buy rating and a $330 price target, implying about 27% upside over the next year. The firm points to the cloud software giant's ai">expanding AI ambitions, specifically its Agentforce platform, as the most important catalyst over the medium term.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Agentforce is Salesforce's vision for "agentic" AI in the front office, where automated agents can take action and not just give recommendations.

AI Tailwinds Build as Salesforce Works Through Early Challenges

Goldman's industry checks suggest that customers are responding well to Agentforce and that Salesforce is gradually removing early adoption hurdles.

Instead of treating Agentforce as a make‑or‑break product, the firm expects it to add about 4% revenue growth to the Platform and Other segment over the next 12 to 18 months, potentially enough to help Salesforce's overall revenue pick up again by 2026.

Looking further ahead, Goldman expects Agentforce to add about 2% yearly growth to Salesforce's overall business between FY26 and FY30. The firm remains positive on the stock despite challenges such as market share losses in Commerce and Marketing Cloud and changes in data‑architecture strategies that affect Data 360.

Despite those headwinds, Goldman still sees a positive outlook. With customer momentum expected to improve through FY27 and a clearer path for AI-driven growth, the firm believes Salesforce is well-positioned to outperform in the coming years.

Is CRM a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, CRM stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 31 Buys, 10 Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $326.95, the average Salesforce stock price target implies a 26.02% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: U.S. Stocks Face 'Near-Term Underperformance' on Fed Independence Risks, Says JPMorgan
data.description:
The trump">Department of Justice's investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell has sparked concerns that the central bank's independence is at risk. That could be problematic for U.S. stocks, according to JPMorgan (JPM).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The risk around Fed independence is likely to push the US toward near-term underperformance," said Andrew Tyler, JPMorgan's Global Head of Market Intelligence.

DOJ Probe Poses Risk to Fed Credibility, Stocks

The Fed's primary goal is to maximize employment and keep inflation contained. However, bowing to political pressure could undermine its credibility and ability to manage the economy effectively.

Tyler said that the investigation creates an "overhang" for U.S. stocks, despite a positive macroeconomic backdrop and strong corporate fundamentals. He added that a buying opportunity could emerge if the Trump administration eases its challenge to Fed independence.
